evaluate and establish the validity of the disbursements;
14.10.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all
documentary requirements supporting all procurement and
disbursements are present; and
14.10.3 Processing and payment of claims that are not properly
documented should be discontinued.

15.3 We recommended that:
15.3.1 The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant – Designate to
submit the necessary supporting documents for the liquidation of
cash advances not signed by the payees;
15.3.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that wages of
employees be given to the directly the payee unless the required
written authority is submitted and require payees to sign on the
payroll register upon receipt.

16.11 We recommended that management:
16.11.1 Submit to the audit team for verification copies of the
Memorandum of Agreement for the above mentioned fund
transfers; and
16.11.2 Henceforth, all transactions which involve funds from other
Government Agencies should be fully supported with a
Memorandum of Agreement or any appropriate documents and
SB Resolution authorizing the Mayor to enter to such
agreements.

17.6 We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
to:
17.6.1 Submit justification on the noted lapses on the ABC computation;
and
17.6.2 Ensure that the latest DPWH Department Order on ABC and
appropriate rates are used in the computation of the mark-up
portion of the ABC of projects.

18.6 We recommended that:
18.6.1 Management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as
guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and
furnish COA of the same for audit purposes.
18.6.2 Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in
accordance with the approved APP of the municipality.

19.5 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC):
19.5.1 Submit justification as to the non-usage of the standard PBDs for
the contract Concreting and Stone Masonry of Farm to Market
Road in Brgy Cabaroan and Basbasa, Tayum, Abra and the
acceptance of the Financial Bid Form not in accordance with the
standard PBDs; and
19.5.2 Together with the Technical Working Group and BAC secretariat
ensure the use of the standard forms (5th Edition) of the PBDs for
succeeding procurement.

20.2 We recommended that the BAC invite observers in writing in all stages of
the procurement process specified in accordance with the provisions of the
2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

21.1.4 We recommended that management:
21.1.4.1 submit the ABC for the above-mentioned infrastructure
project; and
21.1.4.2 prepare ABC in accordance with DPWH Department
Order No. 197, Series of 2016 or its updated issuances on
the matter in its succeeding computation of the ABC of
infrastructure projects for CY 2019 onwards.

21.2.5 We recommended that the BAC submit justification on the noted
observation and henceforth, conduct pre-procurement conference
for procurement that fall under the requirement under Section 20 of
the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

21.3.4 We recommended that the BAC submit justification on the noted
lapses and henceforth, conduct pre-bid conference for procurement
with ABC of ₱1,000,000.00 or above.

21.4.4 We recommended that the members of the BAC submit explanation
on the noted violations and henceforth, abide by the procedures for
post-qualification as provided in the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

21.5 We recommended that the BAC submit explanation on the noted
violations and henceforth, carefully evaluate the prospective
bidders complete eligibility and technical requirements to ensure
that projects are awarded to qualified contractors.

22.5 We recommended that management submit the complete bidding and
contract documents enumerated in item 22.3 and 22.4 (except those already
submitted) pertaining to the procured disaster risk response and rescue
vehicle to the audit team for evaluation of the validity, legality and
propriety of the transaction.

23.5 We recommended that management submit immediately the approved list
of programs and projects to be implemented by the agency from its Share
from Tobacco Excise Tax and Burley and Native Tobacco Excise Tax
received in CY 2018, for the audit team’s evaluation.

24.4 We recommended and management agreed that the municipal accountant
maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the
share from tobacco excise tax to facilitate monitoring of the
implementation and utilization of the fund.

25.4 We recommended that management:
25.4.1 Justify the noted lapses on the disbursements from SEF;
25.4.2 Submit the lacking documents discussed in item 25.3;
25.4.3 Henceforth, require the LSB to submit copy of the SEF budget as
basis for disbursement and certification of availability of funds duly
supported with the DepEd and ECCD Council certification on the
non-funding of the projects/programs/activities to their respective
budgets; and
25.4.4 Ensure that all documentary requirements supporting the
disbursements are present prior payment.

26.3 We recommended that management submit:
26.3.1 The Sanggunian Panlalawigan reviewed appropriation ordinance/
supplemental budget for the unexpended CY 2017 development
fund, if any; and
26.3.2 Complete and accurate utilization report of the 20% Development
Fund for a reliable source of information and guide for all
stakeholders.

27.6 We recommended that the:
27.6.1 Municipal Accountant-Designate maintain a special account and
complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund
to facilitate monitoring of development projects. Operations of the
fund should be accounted for through the maintenance of books of
accounts/complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of
financial statements at any given time; and
27.6.2 Municipal Treasurer transfer the entire 20% development fund to
facilitate monitoring of its utilization and ensure that the fund is
not used for unauthorized programs, projects and activities.

28.5 We recommended that management:
28.5.1 Submit valid justification why it did not seek authority from the
DILG in procuring the DRRR vehicle.
28.5.2 Inquire from the DILG on the next appropriate actions since the
procurement was already made.
28.5.3 Submit to the audit team copies of the result of evaluation by the
DILG as well as reports and documents relative to the procedures to
be taken up.

29.3 We recommended that the LGU submit:
29.3.1 The lists of recipients with their address/ acknowledgement and
quantity/specifics of goods/service received.
29.3.2 Details pertaining to the location/address of the affected areas in
which the goods were distributed for further evaluation.

30.6 We recommended that management submit:
30.6.1 The approved LDRRMFIP, local development plan and annual/
supplemental budget to justify the disbursement made; and
30.6.2 The LDRRM Officer to carefully formulate the LDRRMFIP for the
succeeding years, which also includes programs, projects and
activities to be funded out by unexpended LDRRMF from previous
years.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the
balances of PPE accounts and so as to forestall any losses, determine the
actual existence and condition of properties, the Municipal Mayor form a
committee which will oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and
reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of
accounts. Moreover, submit to the audit team the result of the inventory
through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for
verification.

2.9 From the foregoing, we have recommended the following:
 that cash advances be granted, utilized and liquidated in accordance
with COA Circular No. 97-002. For this, make sure that cash advances
are granted only to authorized officers, properly utilized and liquidated
accordingly;
 that the Municipal Treasurer be instructed to suspend the payment of
salaries of the officials and employees concerned for the restitution of
unliquidated cash advances;
 that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters to those former
employees with outstanding cash advances requesting for the liquidation
or refund of cash advances granted to them; and
 that the disbursement vouchers pertaining to the grant of cash advances
be submitted to the Auditor for proper action. For the cash advances
where the person liable is already deceased, management may request
for authority to write off the amount in accordance with the provisions
of COA Memorandum No. 2002-053 and Rule VI of the 2009 Revised
Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit.

5.5 We have recommended that management undertakes the following:
 Instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to confirm existence of the
livestock and to prepare a status report which includes among others
the number of their offspring and the number of deaths as of December
31, 2016;
 Submit copies of Project Proposal and Memorandum of Agreement
between the Municipal Government and the recipients; and
 Thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to record additions for
reason of birth or reduction due to death or disposal of these animals.

6.7 We have recommended that the liquidations for the cash advance granted to
the Municipal Treasurer together with all the supporting documents be
submitted to the Auditor for verification, otherwise, management must
cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until
they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without
prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such
official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218 of the
Revised Penal Code.

7.6 We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly
and properly supported before payment pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No.
1445.

9.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within
the time prescribed by regulations.

13.6 We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution
properly authorizing the Municipality of Tayum to open a separate bank
account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Accordingly, maintain
separate books for the said fund and establish a complete set of subsidiary
ledgers to account for its transactions.

14.10 We have recommended that the management submit a copy of the
Memorandum of Agreement to the audit team in order to verify whether the
specific purpose for which the fund was established was met.
Henceforth, all transactions which involve funds from other Government
Agencies should be fully supported with a Memorandum of Agreement or
any appropriate document.

15.6 In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
 The MPDC/MENRO sees to it that, henceforth, all relevant cost for the
implementation of the SWMP is integrated to the Annual Budget as well as
Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is
hereby advised;
 The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the
reason for the poor implementation of SWM projects; and
 The members of the SWMB revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No.
9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/ monitoring mechanism to
facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs
indicated in the SWMP.

16.5 We have recommended that management make the necessary
clarifications/justifications on the deficiencies noted. Otherwise, the
concerned contractor and the Municipal officials/employees who
prepared, reviewed and approved the program of works/cost estimates,
budget for the contract and plan should be required to refund the excess
amount of the project.

17.5 We have recommended that the Municipality require the contractor to
repair the defects or refund the applicable cost of the deficiencies noted,
otherwise, these will become the personal liability of the officials and
employees who certified that the projects were 100% implemented
according to plans and specifications.

17.6 Henceforth, we have recommended that strict supervision and monitoring
be required at all times during the implementation stage to ensure the
quality of the projects. Any defects noted shall be immediately rectified
and no payment shall be made unless the noted defects are properly
corrected.

19.8 In view of the above noted deficiencies, we have recommended:
 That management identify priority projects to be funded under the
20% development fund with sufficient details and particulars.
Guidelines and policies involving the implementation of these
programs or project should be considered and included in the planning
and budgeting stage;
 Submit the Program of Works as supporting documents to the
Annual Investment Plan to aid in the further analysis of funded
projects vis-a-vis actual expenditures. For lump sum appropriation,
authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained before
implementation;
 That management implement only projects set forth in the Annual
Investment Program. In instances where other priority projects are
identified for implementation, a Sangguniang Bayan resolution should
first be passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual
Investment Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sangguniang
Panlalawigan; and
 That management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer all
amounts allotted for the 20% development fund to the depository
account specifically created for such fund and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain separate books and subsidiary ledgers for
easier monitoring of utilization and to avoid misuse of funds.

20.3 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that
management:
 Justify the noted lapses and henceforth, management familiarized
themselves with the relevant provisions with regards to the utilization
of the LDRRM Funds;
 Push for the enactment of a local ordinance creating the Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management (DRRM) Office including the required
staffing/personnel. Upon enactment consider the immediate hiring of
qualified DRRM Officer and staff;
 Immediate hiring of qualified DRRM Officer and staff; and
 Require the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution for the opening
of a special trust fund account for the unexpended balance of the fund

 The Municipal Budget Officer/MSWDO being the lead persons in
